name: Pointbank
description_short: PointBank is a community bank serving Denton County since 1884, offering personal and business checking, savings, credit cards, and local loan decisions with 9 branches.
description_long:
PointBank is a locally-owned community bank that has served Denton County continuously since 1884, making it the oldest bank in the region. The institution positions itself as a traditional relationship-based lender with deep community ties rather than a large corporate financial institution. With 9 branches across Denton County and a Customer Care Team based locally, PointBank emphasizes personalized service and decision-making authority at the local level.

PointBank offers a comprehensive range of banking services including free e-checking for both personal and business customers (with e-statement enrollment), online banking and treasury management, mobile banking available 24/7, business credit cards, and personal credit cards with rewards programs. They advertise competitive rates including credit cards starting at 11.99% APR and balance transfer promotions at 4.99% introductory APR for 9 months. The bank also features personal and business banking products, though specific details on savings accounts, CDs, and mortgage offerings are not prominently detailed on their homepage.

What distinguishes PointBank is their emphasis on local decision-making, community involvement (including business breakfast networking events), and personal relationship banking. They highlight that "real bank decisions" are made locally rather than at corporate headquarters, and their Customer Care Team answers phones in Denton County. The bank positions community investment and relationship banking as core differentiators in an increasingly digital financial landscape.

As a regional community bank, PointBank offers traditional banking products and local service, but their website lacks detailed rate comparisons, specific product terms, and comprehensive service descriptions that would allow consumers to fully evaluate their offerings against competitors. The accessibility features mentioned (AudioEye toolbar) suggest attention to inclusive design, though the website content itself is relatively sparse on specific product details, APYs for deposit products, and loan terms.
services: Free personal e-checking with no monthly service charge
Personal credit cards with rewards points program
9 physical branch locations across Denton County
Secure online contact forms for sensitive communications
pros: Free e-checking with no monthly service charge and no minimum balance requirement for personal and business customers
Locally-owned and operated since 1884 with loan decisions made in Denton County rather than at corporate headquarters
Customer Care Team based locally in Denton County available by phone at 940-686-7000 or 972-434-3200
9 physical branch locations for in-person banking and relationship management
24/7 mobile banking app and online banking with treasury management tools
Credit card rewards program with points earned on every purchase
Balance transfer promotional rates at 4.99% introductory APR for 9 months
Community involvement through business breakfasts and local networking events
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Denton County only, not suitable for customers requiring multi-state access
Website provides minimal detail on deposit product APYs, loan terms, and fees compared to competitors
No information visible about mortgage products, refinancing, or home lending services on homepage
Credit card APR starts at 11.99%, which is on the higher end compared to many national card issuers
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or financial advisory services
best_for: Local Denton County residents and small business owners seeking relationship-based community banking
Business owners who benefit from networking opportunities and local decision-making authority
Customers who value in-person branch service and knowing their banker by name
Those seeking no-fee checking with straightforward banking without minimum balance requirements
diagnosis: PointBank is best for Denton County residents and small business owners who prioritize local relationship banking, personalized service, and decision-making authority over competitive rates or extensive product variety. The main caveat is their limited geographic footprint and relatively sparse online information about specific rates, terms, and product details, requiring direct contact with local branches for comprehensive account and loan comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
